description_long: This object consists of a handheld digital multimeter with an orange and black casing, accompanied by two test probes—one red and one black—connected by flexible wires. The multimeter features a rotary dial, a digital display, and several input ports for the probes, all visible on its front face. The device is paired with a rectangular, black, hard-shelled carrying case or battery pack, which sits adjacent to the multimeter. The multimeter's surface is smooth and slightly glossy, with visible markings and symbols around the dial for selecting measurement modes. The probes have pointed metal tips for electrical contact, and the wires are insulated in red and black plastic. This type of device is commonly used by electricians and technicians for measuring voltage, current, and resistance in urban environments such as garages, workshops, or on-site electrical panels.
description: A handheld digital multimeter with orange and black casing, attached test probes, and a rectangular black case. The device features a rotary dial, digital display, and insulated wires.
description_view_0: The multimeter is seen from an angled top view, showing the display, rotary dial, and both test probes with wires spread out.
description_view_1: The side view highlights the thickness of the multimeter and the rectangular black case, with the probe tips visible on top.
description_view_2: A rear-side view showing the back of the black case and the multimeter, with wires trailing outward.
description_view_3: A front-facing view of the multimeter, clearly displaying the digital screen, rotary dial, and both probes extending forward.
